The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

1/15. Can you help the Met find a gunman who shot a nine-year-old girl, leaving her with life-changing injuries?
23/80. Martin battles an overgrown garden in Swansea. And in another Welsh property the views are spectacular, even if the property needs some work.
It's family versus friends in this episode from Staffordshire, presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp, with John Cameron and Irita Marriott skippering the teams as they search the stalls.

programmes.show_name: BBC News at One including BBC Newsline
programmes.show_description: The latest national and international news from the BBC.
Cameron wants his dad to grow up and be more of a father figure to him. Can time in the wild help them reconnect - and put an end to Mark's silly behaviour?
13/15. With all the incredible meat, fish and cheese in Provence, it's easy to focus on the savoury food offerings. But Marcus wants to discover what simple sweet treats are on offer.
31/50. Steve Brown helps a couple wanting space for animals to swap urban Gloucestershire for rural Herefordshire and also visits former escapees who now run a thriving organic farm.
11/20. Mark Wright, Karla Pryce and Asher Edwards help Jax, a creative single mum, who is overwhelmed by the clutter in her home.
1/5. Three soap stars from past road trips, Emmerdale's Hayley Tamaddon, Corrie's Will Mellor and EastEnders star Danny Dyer go antique shopping. Who will be the biggest wheeler-dealer?
51/55. The quiz in which contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

Linda makes a bad situation worse, Julie faces a moral dilemma, and Jack fears that Ravi isn't up to the job.
3/4. Autumn has arrived, but the turn in the weather doesn't mean it's any less busy. One of the sure signs that autumn has arrived is the cows coming home to the sheds.

programmes.show_name: Will Robots Take My Job? - Panorama
programmes.show_description: As Silicon Valley plans massive changes in the workplace, reporter Richard Bilton investigates the rise of the robots and what it could mean for all of us.
9/10. The Bowman team embark on a puzzling case when a body is discovered in an Oxford river. Contains some strong language and some upsetting scenes.

Having retired from boxing, former European champion Tommy McCarthy is taking his career from the ring to the stage as he tries to make it big as a stand-up comic. Contains strong language.
8/8. Tender dominates the headlines, sending shockwaves across the media and the government. Contains strong language, some sexual content and some upsetting scenes.

Natasha Raskin Sharp visits Cheltenham Racecourse, where the teams have expert help from Danny Sebastian and Caroline Hawley. Natasha takes a look at the Cheltenham Gold Cup.

Julie and Jason were happily married for 27 years. But Julie's experience of going through menopause nearly tore them apart. Can time in the wild help get their spark back?

...Domestic Abuse - Spotlight. For 50 years, Foyle Women's Aid has given sanctuary to women in Londonderry trying to escape domestic abuse. Jennifer O'Leary uncovers some of their stories.

programmes.show_name: Ben Youngs Investigates: How Safe Is Rugby?
programmes.show_description: Rugby's Ben Youngs asks if there is a link between concussion and long-term brain health. Contains some strong language.
